#!/bin/bash

# Find the postqueue binary
POSTQUEUE_PATH=$(command -v postqueue 2>/dev/null)

if [ -z "$POSTQUEUE_PATH" ]; then
    echo "postqueue command not found on system"
    exit 1
fi

# Define the audit rules file
AUDIT_RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/privileged-postqueue.rules"

# Generate the audit rule for both 32-bit and 64-bit architectures
RULE_B64="-a always,exit -F path=${POSTQUEUE_PATH} -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k privileged-postqueue"
RULE_B32="-a always,exit -F path=${POSTQUEUE_PATH} -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k privileged-postqueue"

# Check if the rule already exists
if [ -f "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE" ]; then
    if grep -q "path=${POSTQUEUE_PATH}" "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE" 2>/dev/null; then
        # Remove existing rules for postqueue to ensure idempotency
        sed -i "\|path=${POSTQUEUE_PATH}|d" "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
    fi
fi

# Add the audit rule to the rules file
echo "$RULE_B64" >> "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"

# Load the audit rules immediately
augenrules --load

# Restart auditd to ensure rules are active
service auditd restart

exit 0

#!/bin/bash

set -e

FAILLOCK_CONF="/etc/security/faillock.conf"
AUDIT_RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/50-faillock.rules"

if [ ! -f "$FAILLOCK_CONF" ]; then
    touch "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
fi

if ! grep -q '^audit' "$FAILLOCK_CONF"; then
    echo "audit" >> "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
elif grep -q '^audit[[:space:]]*$' "$FAILLOCK_CONF" || grep -q '^#.*audit' "$FAILLOCK_CONF"; then
    sed -i 's/^#.*audit.*/audit/' "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
    if ! grep -q '^audit[[:space:]]*$' "$FAILLOCK_CONF"; then
        echo "audit" >> "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
    fi
fi

mkdir -p /etc/audit/rules.d

cat > "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE" << 'EOF'
-w /var/log/faillock -p wa -k faillock
-w /var/run/faillock -p wa -k faillock
EOF

if systemctl is-active --quiet auditd; then
    augenrules --load
    systemctl restart auditd || service auditd restart
fi

systemctl enable auditd 2>/dev/null || true

exit 0

#!/bin/bash

set -e

GRUB_DEFAULTS="/etc/default/grub"
PARAM="page_poison=1"

# Function to check if parameter is in a string
param_exists() {
    echo "$1" | grep -qw "$PARAM"
}

# Backup grub defaults
if [ -f "$GRUB_DEFAULTS" ]; then
    cp -a "$GRUB_DEFAULTS" "${GRUB_DEFAULTS}.bak.$(date +%s)" 2>/dev/null || true
fi

# Read current GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX
if [ -f "$GRUB_DEFAULTS" ]; then
    CURRENT_CMDLINE=$(grep "^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=" "$GRUB_DEFAULTS" | head -1 | sed 's/^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=//' | sed 's/^"\(.*\)"$/\1/')
else
    CURRENT_CMDLINE=""
fi

# Check if parameter already exists
if ! param_exists "$CURRENT_CMDLINE"; then
    # Add the parameter
    if [ -z "$CURRENT_CMDLINE" ]; then
        NEW_CMDLINE="$PARAM"
    else
        NEW_CMDLINE="$CURRENT_CMDLINE $PARAM"
    fi
    
    # Update or add GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX in grub defaults
    if grep -q "^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=" "$GRUB_DEFAULTS" 2>/dev/null; then
        sed -i "s|^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=.*|GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=\"$NEW_CMDLINE\"|" "$GRUB_DEFAULTS"
    else
        echo "GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=\"$NEW_CMDLINE\"" >> "$GRUB_DEFAULTS"
    fi
fi

# Regenerate grub configuration
if [ -f /sys/firmware/efi/fw_platform_size ]; then
    # EFI system
    grub2-mkconfig -o /boot/efi/EFI/redhat/grub.cfg
else
    # BIOS system
    grub2-mkconfig -o /boot/grub2/grub.cfg
fi

# Update kernel command line for currently installed kernels using grubby
for KERNEL in $(rpm -q kernel --qf '%{VERSION}-%{RELEASE}.%{ARCH}\n'); do
    KERNEL_PATH="/boot/vmlinuz-${KERNEL}"
    if [ -f "$KERNEL_PATH" ]; then
        CURRENT_ARGS=$(grubby --info="$KERNEL_PATH" 2>/dev/null | grep "^args=" | sed 's/^args="//' | sed 's/"$//')
        if ! param_exists "$CURRENT_ARGS"; then
            grubby --update-kernel="$KERNEL_PATH" --args="$PARAM"
        fi
    fi
done

# Ensure default kernel has the parameter
DEFAULT_KERNEL=$(grubby --default-kernel)
if [ -n "$DEFAULT_KERNEL" ]; then
    DEFAULT_ARGS=$(grubby --info="$DEFAULT_KERNEL" 2>/dev/null | grep "^args=" | sed 's/^args="//' | sed 's/"$//')
    if ! param_exists "$DEFAULT_ARGS"; then
        grubby --update-kernel="$DEFAULT_KERNEL" --args="$PARAM"
    fi
fi

exit 0

#!/bin/bash

set -e

RSYSLOG_CONF="/etc/rsyslog.conf"
RSYSLOG_D_DIR="/etc/rsyslog.d"
CUSTOM_CONF="${RSYSLOG_D_DIR}/50-auth-daemon-logging.conf"

mkdir -p "${RSYSLOG_D_DIR}"

cat > "${CUSTOM_CONF}" << 'EOF'
# Authentication and privileged authentication messages to secure log
auth,authpriv.*                                 /var/log/secure

# Daemon messages to messages log
daemon.*                                        /var/log/messages
EOF

chmod 0644 "${CUSTOM_CONF}"

if ! systemctl is-enabled rsyslog.service >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    systemctl enable rsyslog.service
fi

if ! systemctl is-active rsyslog.service >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    systemctl start rsyslog.service
else
    systemctl restart rsyslog.service
fi

touch /var/log/secure
touch /var/log/messages
chmod 0600 /var/log/secure
chmod 0644 /var/log/messages

exit 0

generated_script:
#!/bin/bash

SSHD_CONFIG="/etc/ssh/sshd_config"
REKEY_DATA="512M"
REKEY_TIME="1h"

# Backup original config if not already backed up
if [ ! -f "${SSHD_CONFIG}.bak" ]; then
    cp "${SSHD_CONFIG}" "${SSHD_CONFIG}.bak"
fi

# Function to set or update SSH parameter
set_ssh_parameter() {
    local param="$1"
    local value="$2"
    local config_file="$3"
    
    # Check if parameter exists (commented or uncommented)
    if grep -qE "^[#[:space:]]*${param}[[:space:]]" "${config_file}"; then
        # Replace existing line
        sed -i "s|^[#[:space:]]*${param}[[:space:]].*|${param} ${value}|" "${config_file}"
    else
        # Append if not found
        echo "${param} ${value}" >> "${config_file}"
    fi
}

# Set RekeyLimit parameter
set_ssh_parameter "RekeyLimit" "${REKEY_DATA} ${REKEY_TIME}" "${SSHD_CONFIG}"

# Validate sshd configuration
if sshd -t -f "${SSHD_CONFIG}" 2>/dev/null; then
    # Restart sshd service to apply changes
    systemctl restart sshd
else
    echo "SSHD configuration validation failed. Restoring backup."
    cp "${SSHD_CONFIG}.bak" "${SSHD_CONFIG}"
    exit 1
fi

exit 0

#!/bin/bash

AUDIT_RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/sudoers.rules"
AUDIT_DIR="/etc/sudoers.d"
RULE_KEY="sudoers_cfg"

RULE_W="-w ${AUDIT_DIR} -p wa -k ${RULE_KEY}"

if [ ! -d "/etc/audit/rules.d" ]; then
    mkdir -p /etc/audit/rules.d
fi

if [ -f "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" ]; then
    if ! grep -q "^-w ${AUDIT_DIR} -p wa -k ${RULE_KEY}$" "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"; then
        sed -i "\|^-w ${AUDIT_DIR}|d" "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
        echo "${RULE_W}" >> "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
    fi
else
    echo "${RULE_W}" > "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
fi

if auditctl -l 2>/dev/null | grep -q "^-w ${AUDIT_DIR}"; then
    auditctl -W "${AUDIT_DIR}" -p wa -k "${RULE_KEY}" 2>/dev/null || true
fi

auditctl -w "${AUDIT_DIR}" -p wa -k "${RULE_KEY}" 2>/dev/null || true

if command -v augenrules &> /dev/null; then
    augenrules --load 2>/dev/null || true
fi

systemctl is-active --quiet auditd 2>/dev/null && service auditd restart 2>/dev/null || true

exit 0
```

#!/bin/bash

COMMAND="/usr/sbin/setsebool"
RULE_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/privileged-setsebool.rules"

if [ ! -f "$COMMAND" ]; then
    exit 0
fi

ARCH=$(uname -m)
if [ "$ARCH" = "x86_64" ]; then
    ARCHES=("b64" "b32")
elif [ "$ARCH" = "aarch64" ]; then
    ARCHES=("b64")
else
    ARCHES=("b32")
fi

for arch in "${ARCHES[@]}"; do
    RULE_CONTENT+="-a always,exit -F path=${COMMAND} -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F arch=${arch} -k privileged-setsebool"$'\n'
done

mkdir -p /etc/audit/rules.d

if [ -f "$RULE_FILE" ]; then
    EXISTING_CONTENT=$(cat "$RULE_FILE")
    if [ "$EXISTING_CONTENT" = "$RULE_CONTENT" ]; then
        exit 0
    fi
fi

echo -n "$RULE_CONTENT" > "$RULE_FILE"

if command -v augenrules &> /dev/null; then
    augenrules --load
elif command -v service &> /dev/null; then
    service auditd restart
else
    systemctl restart auditd
fi

    "-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S fremovexattr -F auid=0 -k perm_mod"
)

if [ ! -d "${SUDOERS_D}" ]; then
    mkdir -p "${SUDOERS_D}"
    chmod 750 "${SUDOERS_D}"

# Disable IPv4 forwarding at runtime
sysctl -w net.ipv4.ip_forward=0 >/dev/null 2>&1

# Disable IPv4 forwarding for all interfaces at runtime
for interface in /proc/sys/net/ipv4/conf/*/forwarding; do
    echo 0 > "$interface" 2>/dev/null
done

# Ensure persistent configuration in sysctl.conf
SYSCTL_CONF="/etc/sysctl.conf"
if ! grep -q "^net.ipv4.ip_forward" "$SYSCTL_CONF" 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "net.ipv4.ip_forward = 0" >> "$SYSCTL_CONF"
else
    sed -i 's/^net.ipv4.ip_forward.*/net.ipv4.ip_forward = 0/' "$SYSCTL_CONF"
fi

# Ensure persistent configuration in sysctl.d directory
SYSCTL_D_FILE="/etc/sysctl.d/99-disable-ipv4-forwarding.conf"
cat > "$SYSCTL_D_FILE" << 'EOF'
net.ipv4.ip_forward = 0
net.ipv4.conf.all.forwarding = 0
net.ipv4.conf.default.forwarding = 0
EOF

# Apply all sysctl settings
sysctl -p "$SYSCTL_D_FILE" >/dev/null 2>&1
sysctl -p >/dev/null 2>&1

exit 0

set -euo pipefail

SSHD_CONFIG="/etc/ssh/sshd_config"
SSHD_CONFIG_DIR="/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d"
SETTING="PermitRootLogin"
VALUE="no"

backup_config() {
    if [ ! -f "${SSHD_CONFIG}.bak" ]; then
        cp "${SSHD_CONFIG}" "${SSHD_CONFIG}.bak"
    fi
}

remove_conflicting_entries() {
    sed -i '/^[[:space:]]*PermitRootLogin[[:space:]]/d' "${SSHD_CONFIG}"
    
    if [ -d "${SSHD_CONFIG_DIR}" ]; then
        for conf_file in "${SSHD_CONFIG_DIR}"/*.conf; do
            [ -f "${conf_file}" ] || continue
            sed -i '/^[[:space:]]*PermitRootLogin[[:space:]]/d' "${conf_file}"
        done
    fi
}

add_setting() {
    if ! grep -q "^${SETTING}[[:space:]]" "${SSHD_CONFIG}"; then
        echo "${SETTING} ${VALUE}" >> "${SSHD_CONFIG}"
    fi
}

validate_config() {
    sshd -t
}

reload_sshd() {
    systemctl reload sshd
}

backup_config
remove_conflicting_entries
add_setting
validate_config
reload_sshd
```

mkdir -p /etc/audit/rules.d

# Remove any existing rules for this syscall with our key to ensure idempotency
if [ -f "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE" ]; then
    sed -i "/.*-S ${SYSCALL}.*-k ${KEY}.*/d" "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"

echo "-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S ${SYSCALL} -F auid=0 -k ${KEY}" >> "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"

# Load the rules immediately
augenrules --load

# Restart auditd to ensure rules are active
service auditd restart

exit 0

#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

SSSD_CONF="/etc/sssd/sssd.conf"
AUTHSELECT_PROFILE="sssd"
AUTHSELECT_FEATURES="with-smartcard"

# Install required packages
if ! rpm -q sssd sssd-tools opensc pcsc-lite pcsc-lite-ccid &>/dev/null; then
    dnf install -y sssd sssd-tools opensc pcsc-lite pcsc-lite-ccid
fi

# Enable and start pcscd service
systemctl enable pcscd.service
systemctl start pcscd.service || systemctl restart pcscd.service

# Configure authselect with smartcard support
if ! authselect current | grep -q "with-smartcard"; then
    if authselect current &>/dev/null; then
        CURRENT_PROFILE=$(authselect current | head -n1 | awk '{print $3}')
        authselect select "${CURRENT_PROFILE}" "${AUTHSELECT_FEATURES}" --force
    else
        authselect select "${AUTHSELECT_PROFILE}" "${AUTHSELECT_FEATURES}" --force
    fi
fi

# Backup existing SSSD configuration
if [ -f "${SSSD_CONF}" ]; then
    cp -a "${SSSD_CONF}" "${SSSD_CONF}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
fi

# Create or update SSSD configuration
mkdir -p /etc/sssd
cat > "${SSSD_CONF}" <<'EOF'
[sssd]
services = nss, pam
domains = shadowutils
config_file_version = 2

[domain/shadowutils]
id_provider = files

[pam]
pam_cert_auth = True
pam_cert_db_path = /etc/sssd/pki/sssd_auth_ca_db.pem

[nss]
EOF

# Create certificate directory
mkdir -p /etc/sssd/pki
chmod 755 /etc/sssd/pki

# Set proper permissions on sssd.conf
chmod 600 "${SSSD_CONF}"
chown root:root "${SSSD_CONF}"

# Enable certificate authentication in SSSD
if ! grep -q "^pam_cert_auth.*=.*True" "${SSSD_CONF}"; then
    if grep -q "^\[pam\]" "${SSSD_CONF}"; then
        sed -i '/^\[pam\]/a pam_cert_auth = True' "${SSSD_CONF}"
    else
        echo -e "\n[pam]\npam_cert_auth = True" >> "${SSSD_CONF}"
    fi
fi

# Ensure certificate verification is enabled
if ! grep -q "^pam_cert_verification" "${SSSD_CONF}"; then
    sed -i '/^pam_cert_auth/a pam_cert_verification = ocsp_default_responder' "${SSSD_CONF}"
fi

# Enable and restart SSSD
systemctl enable sssd.service
systemctl restart sssd.service

# Ensure smartcard removal action is configured
if [ ! -f /etc/sssd/conf.d/certificate_authentication.conf ]; then
    mkdir -p /etc/sssd/conf.d
    cat > /etc/sssd/conf.d/certificate_authentication.conf <<'EOF'
[pam]
pam_cert_auth = True
EOF
    chmod 600 /etc/sssd/conf.d/certificate_authentication.conf
    chown root:root /etc/sssd/conf.d/certificate_authentication.conf
fi

# Clear SSSD cache to apply new configuration
sssctl cache-expire -E || true

# Verify services are running
systemctl is-active --quiet pcscd.service || systemctl start pcscd.service
systemctl is-active --quiet sssd.service || systemctl start sssd.service

exit 0

#!/bin/bash

SYSCTL_CONF="/etc/sysctl.d/99-disable-icmp-redirects.conf"

# Create or update sysctl configuration file
cat > "$SYSCTL_CONF" << 'EOF'
net.ipv4.conf.all.send_redirects = 0
net.ipv4.conf.default.send_redirects = 0
EOF

# Apply settings immediately to runtime
sysctl -w net.ipv4.conf.all.send_redirects=0 >/dev/null 2>&1
sysctl -w net.ipv4.conf.default.send_redirects=0 >/dev/null 2>&1

# Apply settings to all existing interfaces
for interface in /proc/sys/net/ipv4/conf/*/send_redirects; do
    echo 0 > "$interface" 2>/dev/null
done

# Reload sysctl to ensure persistence
sysctl -p "$SYSCTL_CONF" >/dev/null 2>&1

exit 0

#!/bin/bash

ARCH=$(uname -m)
RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/modules.rules"

case "${ARCH}" in
    x86_64)
        ARCH_LIST="b64 b32"
        ;;
    aarch64)
        ARCH_LIST="b64 b32"
        ;;
    ppc64le)
        ARCH_LIST="b64"
        ;;
    s390x)
        ARCH_LIST="b64"
        ;;
    *)
        ARCH_LIST="b64 b32"
        ;;
esac

cat > "${RULES_FILE}" << 'EOF'
## Record kernel module loading events by non-system users
EOF

for ARCH_BITS in ${ARCH_LIST}; do
    echo "-a always,exit -F arch=${ARCH_BITS} -S init_module,finit_module -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k modules" >> "${RULES_FILE}"
done

echo "-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S delete_module -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k modules" >> "${RULES_FILE}"

if echo "${ARCH_LIST}" | grep -q "b32"; then
    echo "-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S delete_module -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k modules" >> "${RULES_FILE}"
fi

augenrules --load >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

systemctl is-active --quiet auditd && service auditd restart || true

#!/bin/bash

set -e

RSYSLOG_CONF="/etc/rsyslog.conf"
RSYSLOG_D_DIR="/etc/rsyslog.d"
TLS_CONF_FILE="${RSYSLOG_D_DIR}/00-tls.conf"

mkdir -p "${RSYSLOG_D_DIR}"

if ! rpm -q rsyslog-gnutls &>/dev/null; then
    yum install -y rsyslog-gnutls
fi

cat > "${TLS_CONF_FILE}" << 'EOF'
# Load gtls driver for TLS support
$ModLoad imtcp
$DefaultNetstreamDriver gtls
$ActionSendStreamDriverMode 1
$ActionSendStreamDriverAuthMode x509/name
EOF

for conf_file in "${RSYSLOG_CONF}" "${RSYSLOG_D_DIR}"/*.conf; do
    if [ -f "${conf_file}" ] && [ "${conf_file}" != "${TLS_CONF_FILE}" ]; then
        sed -i 's/^\(\s*\*\.\*\s\+@@\?\)\([^:@]*\)\(:[0-9]\+\)\?$/\1\2\3/' "${conf_file}"
        
        if grep -qE '^\s*\*\.\*\s+@@?[^@]' "${conf_file}"; then
            sed -i '/^\s*\*\.\*\s\+@@\?[^@]/s/^\(\s*\*\.\*\s\+\)@\([^@]\)/\1@@\2/' "${conf_file}"
        fi
    fi
done

systemctl restart rsyslog
systemctl enable rsyslog

exit 0

if systemctl is-active --quiet sssd; then
    systemctl restart sssd

    systemctl start sssd

)

for rule in "${RULES[@]}"; do
    grep -q -F "$rule" "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE" && sed -i "\|^${rule}$|d" "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
done

# Add rules to the file
for rule in "${RULES[@]}"; do
    echo "$rule" >> "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
done

augenrules --load

if [ -z "$SEMANAGE_PATH" ]; then
    SEMANAGE_PATH="/usr/sbin/semanage"
fi

if [ ! -x "$SEMANAGE_PATH" ]; then
    echo "Warning: semanage not found or not executable at $SEMANAGE_PATH"
    exit 1
fi

# Create rules directory if it doesn't exist
mkdir -p /etc/audit/rules.d

# Define the audit rules
RULE_1="-a always,exit -F path=${SEMANAGE_PATH} -F perm=x -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k ${RULE_KEY}"
RULE_2="-a always,exit -F path=${SEMANAGE_PATH} -F perm=x -F auid=0 -k ${RULE_KEY}"

# Remove existing rules for semanage from the file if they exist
if [ -f "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE" ]; then
    sed -i "\|path=${SEMANAGE_PATH}.*-k ${RULE_KEY}|d" "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
fi

# Add the rules to the file
echo "$RULE_1" >> "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
echo "$RULE_2" >> "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"

# Load the rules into the running audit system
augenrules --load

# Restart auditd to ensure rules are active
service auditd restart || systemctl restart auditd

exit 0

#!/bin/bash

AUDIT_RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/50-password-store.rules"
AUDIT_CONF_FILE="/etc/audit/auditd.conf"
PASSWORD_FILE="/etc/security/opasswd"
AUDIT_KEY="password_changes"

# Ensure audit package is installed
if ! rpm -q audit &>/dev/null; then
    dnf install -y audit
fi

# Ensure audit service is enabled and started
systemctl enable auditd
systemctl start auditd

# Create audit rules directory if it doesn't exist
mkdir -p /etc/audit/rules.d

# Remove any existing rules for opasswd to ensure idempotency
if [ -f "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE" ]; then
    sed -i '/\/etc\/security\/opasswd/d' "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
fi

# Add audit rules for opasswd file
cat >> "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE" <<EOF
-w /etc/security/opasswd -p wa -k $AUDIT_KEY
EOF

# Also ensure the rule is loaded in the current session via auditctl
# First remove any existing rule for this file
auditctl -W /etc/security/opasswd -p wa -k $AUDIT_KEY 2>/dev/null || true

# Add the rule to current running audit system
auditctl -w /etc/security/opasswd -p wa -k $AUDIT_KEY

# Regenerate audit rules from rules.d directory
if command -v augenrules &>/dev/null; then
    augenrules --load
fi

# Verify the rule is active
auditctl -l | grep -q "/etc/security/opasswd" || {
    echo "Warning: Rule may not be active in current session"
}

exit 0

#!/bin/bash

SSHD_CONFIG="/etc/ssh/sshd_config"
BACKUP_DIR="/var/backups/ssh"
SETTING="IgnoreUserKnownHosts"
VALUE="yes"

mkdir -p "$BACKUP_DIR"

if [ ! -f "${BACKUP_DIR}/sshd_config.original" ]; then
    cp "$SSHD_CONFIG" "${BACKUP_DIR}/sshd_config.original"
fi

cp "$SSHD_CONFIG" "${BACKUP_DIR}/sshd_config.$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S)"

if grep -q "^[[:space:]]*${SETTING}[[:space:]]" "$SSHD_CONFIG"; then
    sed -i "s/^[[:space:]]*${SETTING}[[:space:]]\+.*/${SETTING} ${VALUE}/" "$SSHD_CONFIG"
elif grep -q "^[[:space:]]*#[[:space:]]*${SETTING}[[:space:]]" "$SSHD_CONFIG"; then
    sed -i "s/^[[:space:]]*#[[:space:]]*${SETTING}[[:space:]]\+.*/${SETTING} ${VALUE}/" "$SSHD_CONFIG"
else
    echo "${SETTING} ${VALUE}" >> "$SSHD_CONFIG"
fi

sshd -t

if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    systemctl restart sshd
else
    echo "SSHD configuration test failed. Restoring backup."
    cp "${BACKUP_DIR}/sshd_config.original" "$SSHD_CONFIG"
    exit 1
fi

systemctl enable sshd

exit 0

#!/bin/bash

set -e

AUDIT_RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/30-file-creation-failures.rules"
RULE_KEY="file_creation_failure"
MIN_UID=1000

# Create the audit rules file with the required rules
cat > "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" << EOF
## Record unsuccessful file creation attempts by regular users
-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S creat -F exit=-EACCES -F auid>=${MIN_UID} -F auid!=unset -k ${RULE_KEY}
-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S creat -F exit=-EPERM -F auid>=${MIN_UID} -F auid!=unset -k ${RULE_KEY}
-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S creat -F exit=-EACCES -F auid>=${MIN_UID} -F auid!=unset -k ${RULE_KEY}
-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S creat -F exit=-EPERM -F auid>=${MIN_UID} -F auid!=unset -k ${RULE_KEY}
EOF

# Set proper permissions on the rules file
chmod 640 "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
chown root:root "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"

# Load the rules into the running configuration
augenrules --load

# Restart auditd to ensure rules are active
service auditd restart

exit 0

#!/bin/bash

set -e

GRUB_CONFIG="/etc/default/grub"
GRUB_PARAM="audit=1"

# Ensure grubby is available (should be on RHEL 8)
if ! command -v grubby &> /dev/null; then
    echo "Error: grubby command not found"
    exit 1
fi

# Function to check if audit=1 is already set for a kernel
has_audit_param() {
    local kernel="$1"
    grubby --info="$kernel" | grep -q "args=.*audit=1"
}

# Function to add audit=1 to a specific kernel
add_audit_to_kernel() {
    local kernel="$1"
    if ! has_audit_param "$kernel"; then
        grubby --update-kernel="$kernel" --args="$GRUB_PARAM"
    fi
}

# Get all installed kernels
mapfile -t KERNELS < <(grubby --info=ALL | grep '^kernel=' | cut -d'"' -f2)

# Add audit=1 to all currently installed kernels
for kernel in "${KERNELS[@]}"; do
    add_audit_to_kernel "$kernel"
done

# Ensure audit=1 is in GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX for future kernels
if [ -f "$GRUB_CONFIG" ]; then
    # Check if audit=1 is already in GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX
    if ! grep -q '^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=.*audit=1' "$GRUB_CONFIG"; then
        # Check if GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX exists
        if grep -q '^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=' "$GRUB_CONFIG"; then
            # Add audit=1 to existing GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX
            sed -i '/^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=/ s/"$/ audit=1"/' "$GRUB_CONFIG"
            # Clean up any double spaces
            sed -i '/^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=/ s/  */ /g' "$GRUB_CONFIG"
            # Remove space before closing quote if it exists
            sed -i '/^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=/ s/ "$/"/g' "$GRUB_CONFIG"
        else
            # Add GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX with audit=1
            echo 'GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX="audit=1"' >> "$GRUB_CONFIG"
        fi
    fi
fi

# Use grubby to set default kernel arguments for future kernels
grubby --update-kernel=ALL --args="$GRUB_PARAM" 2>/dev/null || true

exit 0

#!/bin/bash

while IFS=: read -r username _ uid _ _ homedir shell; do
    if [ "$uid" -ge 1000 ] && [ "$uid" -lt 60000 ]; then
        if [ "$shell" != "/sbin/nologin" ] && [ "$shell" != "/bin/false" ] && [ "$shell" != "/usr/sbin/nologin" ]; then
            if [ -d "$homedir" ]; then
                find "$homedir" -mindepth 1 ! -name '.*' \( -type f -o -type d \) -print0 | while IFS= read -r -d '' item; do
                    current_perms=$(stat -c '%a' "$item" 2>/dev/null)
                    if [ -n "$current_perms" ]; then
                        owner_perms=$((current_perms / 100))
                        group_perms=$(((current_perms / 10) % 10))
                        other_perms=$((current_perms % 10))
                        
                        new_owner_perms=$owner_perms
                        if [ $owner_perms -gt 7 ]; then
                            new_owner_perms=7
                        fi
                        
                        new_group_perms=$group_perms
                        if [ $group_perms -gt 5 ]; then
                            new_group_perms=5
                        fi
                        
                        new_other_perms=0
                        
                        if [ $new_owner_perms -ne $owner_perms ] || [ $new_group_perms -ne $group_perms ] || [ $other_perms -ne 0 ]; then
                            new_mode="${new_owner_perms}${new_group_perms}${new_other_perms}"
                            chmod "$new_mode" "$item" 2>/dev/null
                        fi
                    fi
                done
            fi
        fi
    fi
done < /etc/passwd

                        fi
                        
                        new_group_perms=$group_perms

                        fi
                        
                        new_other_perms=0

#!/bin/bash

set -e

PAM_FAILLOCK_CONF="/etc/security/faillock.conf"
PAM_SYSTEM_AUTH="/etc/pam.d/system-auth"
PAM_PASSWORD_AUTH="/etc/pam.d/password-auth"

if [ ! -f "$PAM_FAILLOCK_CONF" ]; then
    touch "$PAM_FAILLOCK_CONF"
fi

if ! grep -q "^even_deny_root" "$PAM_FAILLOCK_CONF"; then
    echo "even_deny_root" >> "$PAM_FAILLOCK_CONF"
fi

if ! grep -q "^deny" "$PAM_FAILLOCK_CONF"; then
    echo "deny = 3" >> "$PAM_FAILLOCK_CONF"
else
    if ! grep -q "^deny[[:space:]]*=" "$PAM_FAILLOCK_CONF"; then
        sed -i 's/^deny[[:space:]]\+\([0-9]\+\)/deny = \1/' "$PAM_FAILLOCK_CONF"
    fi
fi

if ! grep -q "^unlock_time" "$PAM_FAILLOCK_CONF"; then
    echo "unlock_time = 900" >> "$PAM_FAILLOCK_CONF"
else
    if ! grep -q "^unlock_time[[:space:]]*=" "$PAM_FAILLOCK_CONF"; then
        sed -i 's/^unlock_time[[:space:]]\+\([0-9]\+\)/unlock_time = \1/' "$PAM_FAILLOCK_CONF"
    fi
fi

for PAM_FILE in "$PAM_SYSTEM_AUTH" "$PAM_PASSWORD_AUTH"; do
    if [ ! -f "$PAM_FILE" ]; then
        continue
    fi
    
    if ! grep -q "^auth.*pam_faillock.so.*preauth" "$PAM_FILE"; then
        sed -i '/^auth[[:space:]]\+required[[:space:]]\+pam_env.so/a auth        required      pam_faillock.so preauth' "$PAM_FILE"
    fi
    
    if ! grep -q "^auth.*pam_faillock.so.*authfail" "$PAM_FILE"; then
        sed -i '/^auth[[:space:]]\+sufficient[[:space:]]\+pam_unix.so/a auth        [default=die]  pam_faillock.so authfail' "$PAM_FILE"
    fi
    
    if ! grep -q "^account.*pam_faillock.so" "$PAM_FILE"; then
        sed -i '/^account[[:space:]]\+required[[:space:]]\+pam_unix.so/i account     required      pam_faillock.so' "$PAM_FILE"
    fi
done

authselect current &>/dev/null
if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    CURRENT_PROFILE=$(authselect current -r 2>/dev/null | head -1)
    if [ -n "$CURRENT_PROFILE" ]; then
        authselect select "$CURRENT_PROFILE" --force 2>/dev/null || true
    fi
fi

exit 0

AUDIT_RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/setfacl.rules"
AUDIT_RULES_D="/etc/audit/rules.d"

# Ensure audit rules directory exists
mkdir -p "$AUDIT_RULES_D"

# Define the audit rules
RULE1="-a always,exit -F path=/usr/bin/setfacl -F perm=x -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k perm_mod"
RULE2="-a always,exit -F path=/usr/bin/setfacl -F perm=x -F auid=0 -k perm_mod"

# Create or overwrite the rules file with both rules
cat > "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE" << EOF
## Audit setfacl command executions
$RULE1
$RULE2
EOF

# Load the rules into the running audit system
if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    augenrules --load >/dev/null 2>&1
fi

if command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    # Remove any existing setfacl rules to avoid duplicates
    auditctl -l | grep -F "/usr/bin/setfacl" | while read -r line; do
        if [[ "$line" =~ ^-a ]]; then
            auditctl -d $line 2>/dev/null || true
        fi
    done
    
    # Add the new rules
    auditctl $RULE1 2>/dev/null || true
    auditctl $RULE2 2>/dev/null || true
fi

# Restart auditd to ensure rules are loaded (using service or systemctl)
if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    systemctl restart auditd 2>/dev/null || service auditd restart 2>/dev/null || true
else
    service auditd restart 2>/dev/null || true
fi

exit 0

#!/bin/bash

set -e

AUDIT_BACKLOG_LIMIT=8192
GRUB_CONFIG="/etc/default/grub"
GRUBENV_FILE="/boot/grub2/grubenv"

# Check if grubby is available
if ! command -v grubby &> /dev/null; then
    echo "Error: grubby command not found"
    exit 1
fi

# Function to update all installed kernels
update_installed_kernels() {
    local kernels=$(grubby --info=ALL | grep '^kernel=' | cut -d'"' -f2)
    
    for kernel in $kernels; do
        local current_args=$(grubby --info="$kernel" | grep '^args=' | cut -d'"' -f2)
        
        # Check if audit_backlog_limit is already set
        if echo "$current_args" | grep -q "audit_backlog_limit="; then
            # Remove existing audit_backlog_limit setting
            grubby --update-kernel="$kernel" --remove-args="audit_backlog_limit"
        fi
        
        # Add the correct audit_backlog_limit setting
        grubby --update-kernel="$kernel" --args="audit_backlog_limit=$AUDIT_BACKLOG_LIMIT"
    done
}

# Function to update GRUB default configuration for future kernels
update_grub_default() {
    if [ -f "$GRUB_CONFIG" ]; then
        # Check if GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX exists
        if grep -q "^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=" "$GRUB_CONFIG"; then
            local current_cmdline=$(grep "^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=" "$GRUB_CONFIG" | cut -d'"' -f2)
            
            # Remove existing audit_backlog_limit if present
            local new_cmdline=$(echo "$current_cmdline" | sed 's/audit_backlog_limit=[0-9]*//g' | sed 's/  */ /g' | sed 's/^ //;s/ $//')
            
            # Add audit_backlog_limit
            if [ -z "$new_cmdline" ]; then
                new_cmdline="audit_backlog_limit=$AUDIT_BACKLOG_LIMIT"
            else
                new_cmdline="$new_cmdline audit_backlog_limit=$AUDIT_BACKLOG_LIMIT"
            fi
            
            # Update the file
            sed -i "s|^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=.*|GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=\"$new_cmdline\"|" "$GRUB_CONFIG"
        else
            # Add GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX if it doesn't exist
            echo "GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=\"audit_backlog_limit=$AUDIT_BACKLOG_LIMIT\"" >> "$GRUB_CONFIG"
        fi
    fi
}

# Update all currently installed kernels
update_installed_kernels

# Update GRUB default configuration for future kernels
update_grub_default

# Regenerate grub configuration
if [ -f /sys/firmware/efi/fw_platform_size ]; then
    # UEFI system
    grub2-mkconfig -o /boot/efi/EFI/redhat/grub.cfg 2>/dev/null || true
else
    # BIOS system
    grub2-mkconfig -o /boot/grub2/grub.cfg 2>/dev/null || true
fi

echo "Audit backlog limit set to $AUDIT_BACKLOG_LIMIT for all kernels"

#!/bin/bash

set -e

# Install AIDE if not present
if ! rpm -q aide >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    dnf install -y aide
fi

# Check if active database exists
if [ ! -f /var/lib/aide/aide.db.gz ]; then
    # Initialize new database
    aide --init
    
    # Move the newly initialized database to the active location
    if [ -f /var/lib/aide/aide.db.new.gz ]; then
        mv /var/lib/aide/aide.db.new.gz /var/lib/aide/aide.db.gz
    fi
else
    # Database exists - verify it's valid by attempting a check
    if ! aide --check >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        # Invalid database - reinitialize
        aide --init
        
        if [ -f /var/lib/aide/aide.db.new.gz ]; then
            mv /var/lib/aide/aide.db.new.gz /var/lib/aide/aide.db.gz
        fi
    fi
fi

exit 0

#!/bin/bash

set -e

MOUNT_POINT="/dev/shm"
MOUNT_OPTIONS="nodev"

apply_immediate_mount() {
    if mountpoint -q "$MOUNT_POINT"; then
        current_opts=$(findmnt -n -o OPTIONS "$MOUNT_POINT")
        if [[ ! "$current_opts" =~ (^|,)nodev(,|$) ]]; then
            mount -o remount,nodev "$MOUNT_POINT"
        fi
    fi
}

apply_persistent_mount() {
    local fstab_entry="tmpfs $MOUNT_POINT tmpfs defaults,nodev 0 0"
    local fstab_found=false
    
    if [ -f /etc/fstab ]; then
        while IFS= read -r line || [ -n "$line" ]; do
            if [[ "$line" =~ ^[[:space:]]*# ]] || [[ -z "$line" ]]; then
                continue
            fi
            
            local fields=($line)
            if [ "${#fields[@]}" -ge 2 ]; then
                local mount_target="${fields[1]}"
                if [ "$mount_target" = "$MOUNT_POINT" ]; then
                    fstab_found=true
                    local opts="${fields[3]:-defaults}"
                    
                    if [[ ! "$opts" =~ (^|,)nodev(,|$) ]]; then
                        if [[ "$opts" == "defaults" ]]; then
                            opts="defaults,nodev"
                        else
                            opts="$opts,nodev"
                        fi
                        
                        local new_line="${fields[0]} ${fields[1]} ${fields[2]} $opts"
                        [ "${#fields[@]}" -ge 5 ] && new_line="$new_line ${fields[4]}" || new_line="$new_line 0"
                        [ "${#fields[@]}" -ge 6 ] && new_line="$new_line ${fields[5]}" || new_line="$new_line 0"
                        
                        sed -i "\|^\s*${fields[0]}\s\+${fields[1]}\s|s|.*|$new_line|" /etc/fstab
                    fi
                    break
                fi
            fi
        done < /etc/fstab
    fi
    
    if [ "$fstab_found" = false ]; then
        echo "$fstab_entry" >> /etc/fstab
    fi
}

CHRONY_CONF="/etc/chrony.conf"
MAX_POLL_VALUE=10

    echo "Error: $CHRONY_CONF not found"
    exit 1
fi

cp "$CHRONY_CONF" "${CHRONY_CONF}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S)"

while IFS= read -r line; do
    if [[ "$line" =~ ^[[:space:]]*(server|pool|peer)[[:space:]]+([^[:space:]]+)(.*)$ ]]; then
        directive="${BASH_REMATCH[1]}"
        source="${BASH_REMATCH[2]}"
        options="${BASH_REMATCH[3]}"
        
        if [[ ! "$options" =~ maxpoll[[:space:]]+[0-9]+ ]]; then
            options=$(echo "$options" | sed 's/[[:space:]]*$//')
            if [ -n "$options" ]; then
                echo "$directive $source$options maxpoll $MAX_POLL_VALUE"
            else
                echo "$directive $source maxpoll $MAX_POLL_VALUE"
            fi
        else
            echo "$line"
        fi
    else
        echo "$line"
    fi
done < "$CHRONY_CONF" > "${CHRONY_CONF}.tmp"

mv "${CHRONY_CONF}.tmp" "$CHRONY_CONF"

chmod 644 "$CHRONY_CONF"

if systemctl is-active --quiet chronyd; then
    systemctl restart chronyd
fi

exit 0

#!/bin/bash

LOCKS_DIR="/etc/dconf/db/local.d/locks"
PROFILE_DIR="/etc/dconf/profile"
DB_DIR="/etc/dconf/db/local.d"

mkdir -p "${LOCKS_DIR}"
mkdir -p "${PROFILE_DIR}"
mkdir -p "${DB_DIR}"

cat > "${PROFILE_DIR}/user" <<'EOF'
user-db:user
system-db:local
EOF

cat > "${DB_DIR}/00-screensaver" <<'EOF'
[org/gnome/desktop/session]
idle-delay=uint32 900

[org/gnome/desktop/screensaver]
lock-enabled=true
lock-delay=uint32 0
EOF

cat > "${LOCKS_DIR}/00-screensaver-lock" <<'EOF'
/org/gnome/desktop/session/idle-delay
/org/gnome/desktop/screensaver/lock-enabled
/org/gnome/desktop/screensaver/lock-delay
EOF

dconf update

if [ -n "${DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS}" ]; then
    dconf update
fi

for user_dir in /run/user/*/; do
    if [ -d "${user_dir}" ]; then
        user_id=$(basename "${user_dir}")
        if [ "${user_id}" -ge 1000 ] 2>/dev/null; then
            sudo -u "#${user_id}" DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS="unix:path=/run/user/${user_id}/bus" dconf update 2>/dev/null || true
        fi
    fi
done

chmod 644 "${PROFILE_DIR}/user"
chmod 644 "${DB_DIR}/00-screensaver"
chmod 644 "${LOCKS_DIR}/00-screensaver-lock"
```

    sed -i 's/^SHA_CRYPT_MIN_ROUNDS.*/SHA_CRYPT_MIN_ROUNDS 5000/' "$CONFIG_FILE"
else
    echo "SHA_CRYPT_MIN_ROUNDS 5000" >> "$CONFIG_FILE"
fi

if grep -q "^SHA_CRYPT_MAX_ROUNDS" "$CONFIG_FILE"; then
    sed -i 's/^SHA_CRYPT_MAX_ROUNDS.*/SHA_CRYPT_MAX_ROUNDS 5000/' "$CONFIG_FILE"
else
    echo "SHA_CRYPT_MAX_ROUNDS 5000" >> "$CONFIG_FILE"
fi

# Ensure libuser uses correct rounds in /etc/libuser.conf
LIBUSER_FILE="/etc/libuser.conf"
if [ -f "$LIBUSER_FILE" ]; then
    if grep -q "^\[defaults\]" "$LIBUSER_FILE"; then
        if grep -q "^crypt_style" "$LIBUSER_FILE"; then
            sed -i '/^\[defaults\]/,/^\[/ s/^crypt_style.*/crypt_style = sha512/' "$LIBUSER_FILE"
        else

        fi
    else
        echo -e "[defaults]\ncrypt_style = sha512" >> "$LIBUSER_FILE"
    fi
fi

# Update PAM configuration for password hashing
PAM_SYSTEM_AUTH="/etc/pam.d/system-auth"
PAM_PASSWORD_AUTH="/etc/pam.d/password-auth"

for PAM_FILE in "$PAM_SYSTEM_AUTH" "$PAM_PASSWORD_AUTH"; do

                sed -i -E 's/(^password\s+sufficient\s+pam_unix\.so.*)/\1 sha512/' "$PAM_FILE"
            fi
        fi
    fi
done

exit 0

#!/bin/bash

set -euo pipefail

SSHD_CONFIG="/etc/ssh/sshd_config"
CRYPTO_POLICY_DIR="/etc/crypto-policies/back-ends"
OPENSSHSERVER_CONFIG="${CRYPTO_POLICY_DIR}/opensshserver.config"
FIPS_CIPHERS="aes256-gcm@openssh.com,aes128-gcm@openssh.com,aes256-ctr,aes192-ctr,aes128-ctr"

# Ensure crypto-policies package is installed
if ! rpm -q crypto-policies &>/dev/null; then
    dnf install -y crypto-policies
fi

# Set system-wide crypto policy to FIPS if not already set
current_policy=$(update-crypto-policies --show 2>/dev/null || echo "DEFAULT")
if [[ "${current_policy}" != "FIPS" ]]; then
    update-crypto-policies --set FIPS
fi

# Ensure the crypto-policy back-end directory exists
mkdir -p "${CRYPTO_POLICY_DIR}"

# Create or update the opensshserver.config with FIPS ciphers
cat > "${OPENSSHSERVER_CONFIG}" <<EOF
CRYPTO_POLICY='-oCiphers=${FIPS_CIPHERS}'
EOF

chmod 644 "${OPENSSHSERVER_CONFIG}"

# Remove any local Ciphers override from sshd_config
if grep -q "^[[:space:]]*Ciphers" "${SSHD_CONFIG}"; then
    sed -i '/^[[:space:]]*Ciphers/d' "${SSHD_CONFIG}"
fi

# Ensure sshd_config does not have commented Ciphers that might cause confusion
sed -i '/^[[:space:]]*#[[:space:]]*Ciphers/d' "${SSHD_CONFIG}"

# Validate sshd configuration
sshd -t

# Restart sshd to apply changes
systemctl restart sshd

# Ensure sshd is enabled to start on boot
systemctl enable sshd

exit 0

                fi
            fi
        fi
    fi

Unauthorized access to this system is forbidden and will be prosecuted by law.
By accessing this system, you agree that your actions may be monitored.
EOF

if [ ! -f "${SSHD_CONFIG}.bak" ]; then

# Add Banner directive at the end of the file
echo "Banner $BANNER_FILE" >> "$SSHD_CONFIG"

# Restart sshd service to apply changes
systemctl restart sshd

# Enable sshd to start on boot
systemctl enable sshd

#!/bin/bash

set -e

MOUNT_POINT="/dev/shm"
MOUNT_OPTIONS="nosuid,nodev,noexec"
FSTAB_FILE="/etc/fstab"

# Check if /dev/shm is currently mounted
if ! mountpoint -q "$MOUNT_POINT"; then
    echo "Error: $MOUNT_POINT is not mounted"
    exit 1
fi

# Get current mount options
CURRENT_OPTIONS=$(findmnt -n -o OPTIONS "$MOUNT_POINT")

# Check if nosuid is already present in current mount
if echo "$CURRENT_OPTIONS" | grep -q "nosuid"; then
    NOSUID_PRESENT=true
else
    NOSUID_PRESENT=false
fi

# Remount with nosuid option if not present
if [ "$NOSUID_PRESENT" = false ]; then
    mount -o remount,nosuid "$MOUNT_POINT"
fi

# Update /etc/fstab for persistence
if grep -q "^[^#].*[[:space:]]$MOUNT_POINT[[:space:]]" "$FSTAB_FILE"; then
    # Entry exists, update it
    if grep "^[^#].*[[:space:]]$MOUNT_POINT[[:space:]]" "$FSTAB_FILE" | grep -q "nosuid"; then
        : # nosuid already in fstab
    else
        # Add nosuid to existing options
        sed -i.bak "/^[^#].*[[:space:]]$(echo $MOUNT_POINT | sed 's/\//\\\//g')[[:space:]]/ s/\([[:space:]][^[:space:]]*[[:space:]][^[:space:]]*[[:space:]]\)\([^[:space:]]*\)/\1\2,nosuid/" "$FSTAB_FILE"
    fi
else
    # No entry exists, add one
    echo "tmpfs $MOUNT_POINT tmpfs defaults,nosuid 0 0" >> "$FSTAB_FILE"
fi

# Verify the mount is now protected
FINAL_OPTIONS=$(findmnt -n -o OPTIONS "$MOUNT_POINT")
if echo "$FINAL_OPTIONS" | grep -q "nosuid"; then
    echo "SUCCESS: $MOUNT_POINT is now mounted with nosuid option"
    exit 0
else
    echo "ERROR: Failed to apply nosuid option to $MOUNT_POINT"
    exit 1
fi

#!/bin/bash

for user_line in $(awk -F: '($3>=1000)&&($3!=65534)&&($7!~/nologin|false/)' /etc/passwd); do
    username=$(echo "$user_line" | cut -d: -f1)
    user_home=$(echo "$user_line" | cut -d: -f6)
    user_gid=$(echo "$user_line" | cut -d: -f4)
    user_uid=$(echo "$user_line" | cut -d: -f3)

        user_groups=$(id -G "$username" 2>/dev/null)

                current_gid=$(stat -c '%g' "$item" 2>/dev/null)
                
                if [ -n "$current_gid" ]; then
                    is_member=0
                    for gid in $user_groups; do
                        if [ "$current_gid" = "$gid" ]; then
                            is_member=1
                            break
                        fi
                    done
                    
                    if [ $is_member -eq 0 ]; then
                        chgrp "$user_gid" "$item" 2>/dev/null
                    fi
                fi
            done
        fi
    fi
done

#!/bin/bash

set -euo pipefail

GRUB_CONFIG="/etc/default/grub"
KERNEL_PARAM="vsyscall=none"

if [ ! -f "$GRUB_CONFIG" ]; then
    echo "Error: $GRUB_CONFIG not found"
    exit 1
fi

if grep -q "GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX.*${KERNEL_PARAM}" "$GRUB_CONFIG"; then
    echo "vsyscall=none already present in GRUB configuration"
else
    if grep -q '^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX="' "$GRUB_CONFIG"; then
        sed -i '/^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX="/ s/"$/ '"${KERNEL_PARAM}"'"/' "$GRUB_CONFIG"

        sed -i "/^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX='/ s/'$/ ${KERNEL_PARAM}'/" "$GRUB_CONFIG"
    else
        echo "GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=\"${KERNEL_PARAM}\"" >> "$GRUB_CONFIG"
    fi
    echo "Added vsyscall=none to GRUB configuration"
fi

if [ -d /sys/firmware/efi ]; then
    grub2-mkconfig -o /boot/efi/EFI/redhat/grub.cfg
else
    grub2-mkconfig -o /boot/grub2/grub.cfg
fi

for kernel in $(rpm -qa | grep "^kernel-[0-9]" | sed 's/^kernel-//'); do
    grubby --update-kernel="/boot/vmlinuz-${kernel}" --args="${KERNEL_PARAM}" 2>/dev/null || true
done

grubby --update-kernel=ALL --args="${KERNEL_PARAM}"

echo "vsyscall disabled successfully. Changes will take effect on next reboot."

    fi

fi

if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then

    if [ -n "$CURRENT_PROFILE" ]; then

            fi
        done
    fi
fi

exit 0

#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

PAM_FILE="/etc/pam.d/password-auth"
FAILLOCK_CONF="/etc/security/faillock.conf"

# Ensure faillock.conf exists and has proper settings
if [ ! -f "$FAILLOCK_CONF" ]; then
    touch "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
fi

# Configure faillock parameters
grep -q "^deny" "$FAILLOCK_CONF" 2>/dev/null || echo "deny = 5" >> "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
grep -q "^unlock_time" "$FAILLOCK_CONF" 2>/dev/null || echo "unlock_time = 900" >> "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
grep -q "^fail_interval" "$FAILLOCK_CONF" 2>/dev/null || echo "fail_interval = 900" >> "$FAILLOCK_CONF"

sed -i 's/^deny[[:space:]]*=.*/deny = 5/' "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
sed -i 's/^unlock_time[[:space:]]*=.*/unlock_time = 900/' "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
sed -i 's/^fail_interval[[:space:]]*=.*/fail_interval = 900/' "$FAILLOCK_CONF"

# Backup PAM file
cp -f "$PAM_FILE" "${PAM_FILE}.bak.$(date +%s)"

# Remove any existing pam_faillock lines to avoid duplicates
sed -i '/pam_faillock\.so/d' "$PAM_FILE"

# Find the first auth line and add pam_faillock preauth before it
if ! grep -q "^auth[[:space:]]*required[[:space:]]*pam_faillock\.so preauth" "$PAM_FILE"; then
    sed -i '0,/^auth/{/^auth/i auth        required      pam_faillock.so preauth
}' "$PAM_FILE"
fi

# Add pam_faillock authfail after pam_unix.so in auth section
if ! grep -q "^auth[[:space:]]*\[default=die\][[:space:]]*pam_faillock\.so authfail" "$PAM_FILE"; then
    sed -i '/^auth[[:space:]]*.*pam_unix\.so/a auth        [default=die] pam_faillock.so authfail' "$PAM_FILE"
fi

# Add pam_faillock in account section before pam_unix.so if not present
if ! grep -q "^account[[:space:]]*required[[:space:]]*pam_faillock\.so" "$PAM_FILE"; then
    sed -i '0,/^account[[:space:]]*.*pam_unix\.so/{/^account[[:space:]]*.*pam_unix\.so/i account     required      pam_faillock.so
}' "$PAM_FILE"
fi

#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

PAM_FILE="/etc/pam.d/password-auth"
FAILLOCK_CONF="/etc/security/faillock.conf"

# Ensure faillock.conf exists and has proper settings
if [ ! -f "$FAILLOCK_CONF" ]; then
    touch "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
fi

# Configure faillock parameters
grep -q "^deny" "$FAILLOCK_CONF" 2>/dev/null || echo "deny = 5" >> "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
grep -q "^unlock_time" "$FAILLOCK_CONF" 2>/dev/null || echo "unlock_time = 900" >> "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
grep -q "^fail_interval" "$FAILLOCK_CONF" 2>/dev/null || echo "fail_interval = 900" >> "$FAILLOCK_CONF"

sed -i 's/^deny[[:space:]]*=.*/deny = 5/' "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
sed -i 's/^unlock_time[[:space:]]*=.*/unlock_time = 900/' "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
sed -i 's/^fail_interval[[:space:]]*=.*/fail_interval = 900/' "$FAILLOCK_CONF"

# Backup PAM file
cp -f "$PAM_FILE" "${PAM_FILE}.bak.$(date +%s)"

# Remove any existing pam_faillock lines to avoid duplicates
sed -i '/pam_faillock\.so/d' "$PAM_FILE"

# Find the first auth line and add pam_faillock preauth before it
if ! grep -q "^auth[[:space:]]*required[[:space:]]*pam_faillock\.so preauth" "$PAM_FILE"; then
    sed -i '0,/^auth/{/^auth/i auth        required      pam_faillock.so preauth
}' "$PAM_FILE"
fi

# Add pam_faillock authfail after pam_unix.so in auth section
if ! grep -q "^auth[[:space:]]*\[default=die\][[:space:]]*pam_faillock\.so authfail" "$PAM_FILE"; then
    sed -i '/^auth[[:space:]]*.*pam_unix\.so/a auth        [default=die] pam_faillock.so authfail' "$PAM_FILE"
fi

# Add pam_faillock in account section before pam_unix.so if not present
if ! grep -q "^account[[:space:]]*required[[:space:]]*pam_faillock\.so" "$PAM_FILE"; then
    sed -i '0,/^account[[:space:]]*.*pam_unix\.so/{/^account[[:space:]]*.*pam_unix\.so/i account     required      pam_faillock.so
}' "$PAM_FILE"
fi

# Create symlink for system-auth to use same configuration
if [ -f /etc/pam.d/system-auth ] && [ ! -L /etc/pam.d/system-auth ]; then
    cp -f /etc/pam.d/system-auth /etc/pam.d/system-auth.bak.$(date +%s)
    sed -i '/pam_faillock\.so/d' /etc/pam.d/system-auth
    
    if ! grep -q "^auth[[:space:]]*required[[:space:]]*pam_faillock\.so preauth" /etc/pam.d/system-auth; then
        sed -i '0,/^auth/{/^auth/i auth        required      pam_faillock.so preauth
}' /etc/pam.d/system-auth
    fi

        sed -i '/^auth[[:space:]]*.*pam_unix\.so/a auth        [default=die] pam_faillock.so authfail' /etc/pam.d/system-auth

}' /etc/pam.d/system-auth

fi

mkdir -p /etc/dconf/profile

user-db:user
system-db:local
EOF

# Create dconf local database directory
mkdir -p /etc/dconf/db/local.d

# Create or overwrite the setting to disable user list
cat > /etc/dconf/db/local.d/00-security-settings << 'EOF'

EOF

# Create locks directory
mkdir -p /etc/dconf/db/local.d/locks

# Create or overwrite lock file to prevent user override
cat > /etc/dconf/db/local.d/locks/00-security-settings-lock << 'EOF'
/org/gnome/login-screen/disable-user-list
EOF

# Update dconf database
dconf update

# Restart gdm if it's running to apply changes immediately
if systemctl is-active --quiet gdm; then
    systemctl restart gdm
fi

sysctl -w net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_ra=0 >/dev/null 2>&1

# Ensure persistent configuration
mkdir -p "$(dirname "$SYSCTL_CONF")"

cat > "$SYSCTL_CONF" <<EOF
net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_ra=0
net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_ra=0
EOF

# Apply settings from the configuration file
sysctl -p "$SYSCTL_CONF" >/dev/null 2>&1

exit 0
```

fi

    if [ -f "$config" ]; then

    if grep -q "^fail_interval" "$FAILLOCK_CONF"; then
        sed -i 's/^fail_interval[[:space:]]*=.*/fail_interval = 900/' "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
    else
        echo "fail_interval = 900" >> "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
    fi
fi

authselect current > /dev/null 2>&1
if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    authselect apply-changes -b
fi

exit 0
```

#!/bin/bash

set -euo pipefail

declare -a INIT_FILES=(
    ".bashrc"
    ".bash_profile"
    ".bash_login"
    ".profile"
    ".bash_logout"
    ".zshrc"
    ".zprofile"
    ".zlogin"
    ".zlogout"
    ".cshrc"
    ".login"
    ".logout"
    ".kshrc"
)

get_users_with_home() {
    local users=()
    users+=("root")
    
    while IFS=: read -r username _ uid _ _ homedir shell; do
        if [[ $uid -ge 1000 || $uid -eq 0 ]] && \
           [[ -d "$homedir" ]] && \
           [[ "$shell" != */nologin ]] && \
           [[ "$shell" != */false ]] && \
           [[ "$shell" != "/bin/sync" ]]; then
            if [[ "$username" != "root" ]]; then
                users+=("$username:$homedir")
            fi
        fi
    done < /etc/passwd
    
    printf '%s\n' "${users[@]}"
}

is_more_permissive() {
    local current_perms=$1
    local max_perms=0740
    
    local current_owner=$(( (current_perms >> 6) & 7 ))
    local current_group=$(( (current_perms >> 3) & 7 ))
    local current_other=$(( current_perms & 7 ))
    
    local max_owner=$(( (max_perms >> 6) & 7 ))
    local max_group=$(( (max_perms >> 3) & 7 ))
    local max_other=$(( max_perms & 7 ))
    
    if (( (current_owner & ~max_owner) != 0 )); then
        return 0
    fi
    if (( (current_group & ~max_group) != 0 )); then
        return 0
    fi
    if (( current_other != 0 )); then
        return 0
    fi
    
    return 1
}

calculate_new_perms() {
    local current_perms=$1
    
    local current_owner=$(( (current_perms >> 6) & 7 ))
    local current_group=$(( (current_perms >> 3) & 7 ))
    local current_other=$(( current_perms & 7 ))
    
    local new_owner=$current_owner
    if (( (current_owner & 1) != 0 )); then
        new_owner=$(( current_owner & 6 ))
    fi
    
    local new_group=$(( current_group & 4 ))
    
    local new_other=0
    
    local new_perms=$(( (new_owner << 6) | (new_group << 3) | new_other ))
    
    printf '%o' "$new_perms"
}

process_user() {
    local user_info=$1
    local username homedir
    
    if [[ "$user_info" == "root" ]]; then
        username="root"
        homedir=$(getent passwd root | cut -d: -f6)
    else
        username="${user_info%%:*}"
        homedir="${user_info#*:}"
    fi
    
    if [[ ! -d "$homedir" ]]; then
        return
    fi
    
    for init_file in "${INIT_FILES[@]}"; do
        local filepath="$homedir/$init_file"
        
        if [[ -f "$filepath" && ! -L "$filepath" ]]; then
            local current_perms=$(stat -c '%a' "$filepath")
            local current_perms_octal=$((8#$current_perms))
            
            if is_more_permissive "$current_perms_octal"; then
                local new_perms=$(calculate_new_perms "$current_perms_octal")
                chmod "$new_perms" "$filepath"
            fi
        fi
    done
}

main() {
    while IFS= read -r user_info; do
        process_user "$user_info"
    done < <(get_users_with_home)
}

main

#!/bin/bash

AUDIT_RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/sudoers.rules"
SUDOERS_FILE="/etc/sudoers"
AUDIT_KEY="sudoers_changes"

# Create audit rule for sudoers file
AUDIT_RULE="-w ${SUDOERS_FILE} -p wa -k ${AUDIT_KEY}"

# Ensure audit rules directory exists
mkdir -p /etc/audit/rules.d

# Check if rule already exists in the rules file
if [ -f "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" ]; then
    if grep -q "^-w ${SUDOERS_FILE} -p wa -k ${AUDIT_KEY}$" "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"; then
        RULE_EXISTS_IN_FILE=true
    else
        RULE_EXISTS_IN_FILE=false
    fi
else
    RULE_EXISTS_IN_FILE=false
fi

# Add rule to persistent configuration if not present
if [ "${RULE_EXISTS_IN_FILE}" = false ]; then
    echo "${AUDIT_RULE}" >> "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
fi

# Load the rule into the running auditd configuration if not already loaded
if ! auditctl -l | grep -q "^-w ${SUDOERS_FILE} -p wa -k ${AUDIT_KEY}$"; then
    auditctl -w "${SUDOERS_FILE}" -p wa -k "${AUDIT_KEY}"
fi

# Ensure auditd service is enabled and running
systemctl enable auditd
systemctl restart auditd

exit 0

    yum install -y dracut-fips dracut-fips-abicheck
fi

if [ ! -f /proc/sys/crypto/fips_enabled ] || [ "$(cat /proc/sys/crypto/fips_enabled)" != "1" ]; then
    fips-mode-setup --enable
    
    if ! grubby --info=ALL | grep -q "fips=1"; then
        grubby --update-kernel=ALL --args="fips=1"
    fi
    
    BOOT_UUID=$(findmnt -no UUID /boot)
    if [ -n "$BOOT_UUID" ]; then
        if ! grubby --info=ALL | grep -q "boot=UUID=${BOOT_UUID}"; then
            grubby --update-kernel=ALL --args="boot=UUID=${BOOT_UUID}"
        fi
    fi
    
    dracut -f
    
    if [ "$(cat /proc/sys/crypto/fips_enabled)" != "1" ]; then
        echo "System will be in FIPS mode after reboot"
    fi
else
    if ! grubby --info=ALL | grep -q "fips=1"; then
        grubby --update-kernel=ALL --args="fips=1"
    fi
    
    BOOT_UUID=$(findmnt -no UUID /boot)
    if [ -n "$BOOT_UUID" ]; then
        if ! grubby --info=ALL | grep -q "boot=UUID=${BOOT_UUID}"; then

#!/bin/bash

set -e

RULES_DIR="/etc/audit/rules.d"
RULES_FILE="${RULES_DIR}/perm_mod.rules"
AUDITCTL_FILE="/etc/audit/audit.rules"

MIN_UID=$(awk '/^UID_MIN/ {print $2}' /etc/login.defs)
if [[ -z "${MIN_UID}" ]]; then
    MIN_UID=1000
fi

ARCH=$(uname -m)
if [[ "${ARCH}" == "x86_64" ]] || [[ "${ARCH}" == "aarch64" ]]; then
    IS_64BIT=true
else
    IS_64BIT=false
fi

RULE_TAG="perm_mod"

RULE_64="-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S lsetxattr -F auid>=${MIN_UID} -F auid!=unset -k ${RULE_TAG}"
RULE_64_ROOT="-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S lsetxattr -F auid=0 -k ${RULE_TAG}"
RULE_32="-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S lsetxattr -F auid>=${MIN_UID} -F auid!=unset -k ${RULE_TAG}"
RULE_32_ROOT="-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S lsetxattr -F auid=0 -k ${RULE_TAG}"

mkdir -p "${RULES_DIR}"

cat > "${RULES_FILE}" << EOF
## Audit lsetxattr system calls for permission modification detection
EOF

if [[ "${IS_64BIT}" == true ]]; then
    echo "${RULE_64}" >> "${RULES_FILE}"
    echo "${RULE_64_ROOT}" >> "${RULES_FILE}"
fi

echo "${RULE_32}" >> "${RULES_FILE}"
echo "${RULE_32_ROOT}" >> "${RULES_FILE}"

if [[ -f "${AUDITCTL_FILE}" ]]; then
    if [[ "${IS_64BIT}" == true ]]; then
        if ! grep -qF -- "${RULE_64}" "${AUDITCTL_FILE}" 2>/dev/null; then
            echo "${RULE_64}" >> "${AUDITCTL_FILE}"
        fi
        if ! grep -qF -- "${RULE_64_ROOT}" "${AUDITCTL_FILE}" 2>/dev/null; then
            echo "${RULE_64_ROOT}" >> "${AUDITCTL_FILE}"
        fi
    fi
    
    if ! grep -qF -- "${RULE_32}" "${AUDITCTL_FILE}" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "${RULE_32}" >> "${AUDITCTL_FILE}"
    fi
    if ! grep -qF -- "${RULE_32_ROOT}" "${AUDITCTL_FILE}" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "${RULE_32_ROOT}" >> "${AUDITCTL_FILE}"
    fi
fi

if command -v augenrules &> /dev/null; then
    augenrules --load
elif command -v auditctl &> /dev/null; then
    auditctl -R "${AUDITCTL_FILE}" 2>/dev/null || auditctl -R "${RULES_FILE}"
fi

if systemctl is-active --quiet auditd; then
    systemctl restart auditd || service auditd restart
else
    systemctl start auditd || service auditd start
fi

systemctl enable auditd || chkconfig auditd on

exit 0

#!/bin/bash

set -e

AIDE_CONF="/etc/aide.conf"
AIDE_CUSTOM_CONF="/etc/aide.conf.d/audit_tools.conf"
AIDE_DB="/var/lib/aide/aide.db.gz"
AIDE_DB_NEW="/var/lib/aide/aide.db.new.gz"

AUDIT_TOOLS=(
    "/sbin/auditctl"
    "/sbin/aureport"
    "/sbin/ausearch"
    "/sbin/autrace"
    "/sbin/auditd"
    "/sbin/augenrules"
    "/usr/sbin/auditctl"
    "/usr/sbin/aureport"
    "/usr/sbin/ausearch"
    "/usr/sbin/autrace"
    "/usr/sbin/auditd"
    "/usr/sbin/augenrules"
)

if ! rpm -q aide &>/dev/null; then
    yum install -y aide
fi

mkdir -p /etc/aide.conf.d

if ! grep -q "^@@include[[:space:]]\+@@{TOPDIR}/aide.conf.d/audit_tools.conf" "$AIDE_CONF" 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "@@include @@{TOPDIR}/aide.conf.d/audit_tools.conf" >> "$AIDE_CONF"
fi

cat > "$AIDE_CUSTOM_CONF" << 'EOF'
# AIDE configuration for audit tools
# Monitor permissions, ownership, and content hashes

EOF

for tool in "${AUDIT_TOOLS[@]}"; do
    if [ -f "$tool" ]; then
        if ! grep -qF "$tool" "$AIDE_CUSTOM_CONF" 2>/dev/null; then
            echo "$tool p+i+n+u+g+s+b+m+c+md5+sha256+sha512" >> "$AIDE_CUSTOM_CONF"
        fi
    fi
done

if [ ! -f "$AIDE_DB" ]; then
    aide --init
    if [ -f "$AIDE_DB_NEW" ]; then
        mv "$AIDE_DB_NEW" "$AIDE_DB"
    fi
else
    aide --update
    if [ -f "$AIDE_DB_NEW" ]; then
        mv "$AIDE_DB_NEW" "$AIDE_DB"
    fi
fi

exit 0

exit 0

    echo "Error: $GSHADOW_FILE does not exist"
    exit 1

RULE="-w $GSHADOW_FILE -p wa -k $KEY"

if command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if ! auditctl -l | grep -q -- "-w $GSHADOW_FILE -p wa -k $KEY"; then
        auditctl -w "$GSHADOW_FILE" -p wa -k "$KEY"
    fi
fi

if systemctl is-active --quiet auditd 2>/dev/null; then
    systemctl restart auditd || service auditd restart
fi

exit 0

    if [ ! -f "$FAILLOCK_CONF" ]; then
        touch "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
    fi
    
    if ! grep -q "^deny" "$FAILLOCK_CONF"; then
        echo "deny = 5" >> "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
    else
        sed -i 's/^deny[[:space:]]*=.*/deny = 5/' "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
    fi

        echo "unlock_time = 900" >> "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
    else
        sed -i 's/^unlock_time[[:space:]]*=.*/unlock_time = 900/' "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
    fi
    
    if ! grep -q "^fail_interval" "$FAILLOCK_CONF"; then
        echo "fail_interval = 900" >> "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
    else
        sed -i 's/^fail_interval[[:space:]]*=.*/fail_interval = 900/' "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
    fi
}

configure_pam_file() {
    local PAM_FILE="$1"
    
    if [ ! -f "$PAM_FILE" ]; then
        return
    fi
    
    cp "$PAM_FILE" "${PAM_FILE}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
    
    if ! grep -q "^auth[[:space:]]*required[[:space:]]*pam_faillock.so[[:space:]]*preauth" "$PAM_FILE"; then

    fi

    fi
    
    if ! grep -q "^auth[[:space:]]*required[[:space:]]*pam_faillock.so[[:space:]]*authsucc" "$PAM_FILE"; then
        if grep -q "^auth[[:space:]]*\[default=die\][[:space:]]*pam_faillock.so[[:space:]]*authfail" "$PAM_FILE"; then
            sed -i '/^auth[[:space:]]*\[default=die\][[:space:]]*pam_faillock.so[[:space:]]*authfail/a auth        required      pam_faillock.so authsucc' "$PAM_FILE"

    fi
fi

RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/privileged-ssh-keysign.rules"

if [ -f "$RULES_FILE" ]; then
    if grep -Fxq "$AUDIT_RULE" "$RULES_FILE"; then
        RULE_EXISTS_IN_FILE=1
    else

if [ $RULE_EXISTS_IN_FILE -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "$AUDIT_RULE" > "$RULES_FILE"
fi

# Check if rule is currently loaded
if auditctl -l | grep -F 'path=/usr/libexec/openssh/ssh-keysign' | grep -q 'auid>=1000'; then

else

if [ $RULE_LOADED -eq 0 ]; then
    auditctl -a always,exit -F path=/usr/libexec/openssh/ssh-keysign -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k privileged 2>/dev/null || true
fi

#!/bin/bash

SSHD_CONFIG="/etc/ssh/sshd_config"
BACKUP_DIR="/var/backups/sshd"
SETTING="PermitUserEnvironment"
VALUE="no"

mkdir -p "$BACKUP_DIR"

if [ ! -f "${BACKUP_DIR}/sshd_config.original" ]; then
    cp "$SSHD_CONFIG" "${BACKUP_DIR}/sshd_config.original"
fi

cp "$SSHD_CONFIG" "${BACKUP_DIR}/sshd_config.$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S)"

if grep -q "^[[:space:]]*${SETTING}[[:space:]]" "$SSHD_CONFIG"; then
    sed -i "s/^[[:space:]]*${SETTING}[[:space:]].*/PermitUserEnvironment no/" "$SSHD_CONFIG"
else
    echo "PermitUserEnvironment no" >> "$SSHD_CONFIG"
fi

sed -i "/^[[:space:]]*#[[:space:]]*${SETTING}[[:space:]]/d" "$SSHD_CONFIG"

if ! sshd -t -f "$SSHD_CONFIG" 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "ERROR: Invalid SSH configuration. Restoring backup."
    cp "${BACKUP_DIR}/sshd_config.original" "$SSHD_CONFIG"
    exit 1
fi

systemctl reload sshd || systemctl restart sshd

exit 0

    "/var/log/lastlog"
)

        :
    else
        auditctl -w "${file}" -p wa -k logins 2>/dev/null || true

done

    augenrules --load
fi

#!/bin/bash

set -e

UNLOCK_TIME=900
DENY=5
FAIL_INTERVAL=900

configure_pam_faillock() {
    local file="$1"
    
    if [ ! -f "$file" ]; then
        return
    fi
    
    cp "$file" "${file}.bak.$(date +%s)"
    
    if grep -q "^auth.*pam_faillock.so preauth" "$file"; then
        sed -i '/^auth.*pam_faillock.so preauth/d' "$file"
    fi
    if grep -q "^auth.*pam_faillock.so authfail" "$file"; then
        sed -i '/^auth.*pam_faillock.so authfail/d' "$file"
    fi
    if grep -q "^account.*pam_faillock.so" "$file"; then
        sed -i '/^account.*pam_faillock.so/d' "$file"
    fi
    
    local auth_required_line=$(grep -n "^auth.*required.*pam_deny.so" "$file" | head -1 | cut -d: -f1)
    if [ -z "$auth_required_line" ]; then
        auth_required_line=$(grep -n "^auth.*sufficient.*pam_unix.so" "$file" | head -1 | cut -d: -f1)
    fi
    
    if [ -n "$auth_required_line" ]; then
        sed -i "${auth_required_line}i auth        required      pam_faillock.so preauth silent deny=${DENY} unlock_time=${UNLOCK_TIME} fail_interval=${FAIL_INTERVAL}" "$file"
        
        local auth_sufficient_line=$(grep -n "^auth.*sufficient.*pam_unix.so" "$file" | head -1 | cut -d: -f1)
        if [ -n "$auth_sufficient_line" ]; then
            auth_sufficient_line=$((auth_sufficient_line + 1))
            sed -i "${auth_sufficient_line}a auth        [default=die] pam_faillock.so authfail deny=${DENY} unlock_time=${UNLOCK_TIME} fail_interval=${FAIL_INTERVAL}" "$file"
        fi
    fi
    
    local account_required_line=$(grep -n "^account.*required.*pam_unix.so" "$file" | head -1 | cut -d: -f1)
    if [ -n "$account_required_line" ]; then
        sed -i "${account_required_line}i account     required      pam_faillock.so" "$file"
    fi
}

configure_faillock_conf() {
    local conf_file="/etc/security/faillock.conf"
    
    if [ -f "$conf_file" ]; then
        cp "$conf_file" "${conf_file}.bak.$(date +%s)"
        
        if grep -q "^deny" "$conf_file"; then
            sed -i "s/^deny.*/deny = ${DENY}/" "$conf_file"
        else
            echo "deny = ${DENY}" >> "$conf_file"
        fi
        
        if grep -q "^unlock_time" "$conf_file"; then
            sed -i "s/^unlock_time.*/unlock_time = ${UNLOCK_TIME}/" "$conf_file"
        else
            echo "unlock_time = ${UNLOCK_TIME}" >> "$conf_file"
        fi
        
        if grep -q "^fail_interval" "$conf_file"; then
            sed -i "s/^fail_interval.*/fail_interval = ${FAIL_INTERVAL}/" "$conf_file"
        else
            echo "fail_interval = ${FAIL_INTERVAL}" >> "$conf_file"
        fi
    else
        cat > "$conf_file" <<EOF
deny = ${DENY}
unlock_time = ${UNLOCK_TIME}
fail_interval = ${FAIL_INTERVAL}
EOF
    fi
}

configure_pam_faillock "/etc/pam.d/system-auth"
configure_pam_faillock "/etc/pam.d/password-auth"

configure_faillock_conf

authselect current >/dev/null 2>&1
if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    current_profile=$(authselect current | grep "Profile ID" | awk '{print $3}')
    if [ -n "$current_profile" ]; then
        authselect select "$current_profile" --force with-faillock
    fi
fi

exit 0

    current_profile=$(authselect current | grep "Profile ID" | awk '{print $3}')
    if [ -n "$current_profile" ]; then
        authselect select "$current_profile" --force with-faillock
    fi
fi

exit 0
```
